[30;1m313 | [0m[35mNote that calling `enable()` completely overrides previously set DEBUG variable : [0m
[30;1m314 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m315 | [0m[35m```[0m
[30;1m316 | [0m[35m$ DEBUG=foo node -e 'var dbg = require("debug"); dbg.enable("bar"); console.log(dbg.enabled("foo"))'[0m
[30;1m317 | [0m[35m=> false[0m
[30;1m318 | [0m[35m```[0m
[30;1m319 | [0m[35m[0m

[30;1m221 | [0m[35mDebug's enable state is currently persisted by `localStorage`.[0m
[30;1m222 | [0m[35mConsider the situation shown below where you have `worker:a` and `worker:b`,[0m
[30;1m223 | [0m[35mand wish to debug both. You can enable this using `localStorage.debug`:[0m
[30;1m224 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m225 | [0m[35m```js[0m
[30;1m226 | [0m[35mlocalStorage.debug = 'worker:*'[0m

[31m--[ [0m[34mMatch #[0m[33m607[0m[34m of [0m[33m732[0m[31m ]--[0m
